calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder for Beverly Lee (Trustee).

Mr. Feder stated that the trust estate is at about $7,500.00, that there is minimal income, that the original order required accountings be filed pursuant to Probate Code 2628 and that the only income is interest that is being generated on what ever is left in it. Mr. Feder also stated that the gift cards are similar to an allowance, that the idea was that the Trust would provide gifts to the Beneficiary, that they are a strategy that is used when the Trustee can not go with the Beneficiary to purchase things they may need, that Medi-Cal is okay with it, and that the Trustee is doing the best she could to see that her Brother has what he needs and has his independence. The Court stated that $15,000.00 is a lot, that it is a big chunk, that it is unusual and questions what the gift cards are being used for/what is being purchased with them. The Second Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Z. Grimaldi. Nuque. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kimberly Thomas (via court call) for Beverly Lee.

Ms. Thomas addressed the issue of prior attorney fees stating that the last petition had been filed and approved, that subsequently Ms. Lee paid the prior counsel which had not been ordered by the court, that the attorney's were hired to prepare the Special Needs Trust (SNT) only and not the petition. Ms. Thomas gave the court a breakdown of their attorney fees, stated that they filed an amendment to address the examiner's notes, that they had requested that the SNT be closed but then found that Mr. Lee was receiving benefits so asked to keep the SNT intact and that the gift cards are not being used for food or shelter. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
